name: ampel-lsst

metadata.name: ampel-lsst
metadata.summary: Legacy Survey of Space and Time support for the Ampel system

documentation:
# LSST plugin for AMPEL

LSST-specific implementations for Ampel such as: - An _AlertSupplier_ compatible with plasticc generated alerts - Shaper classes for ingestion

classes.name: ampel.lsst.t1.LSSTT1Combiner.LSSTT1Combiner
documentation: Dummy class that exists only to provide default values for access and policy

param arg: sequence of unshaped dps
---

classes.name: ampel.lsst.t2.T2GetAlertId.T2GetAlertId
documentation: Get first alertId associated with the latest datapoint in a T1

classes.name: ampel.lsst.ingest.LSSTMongoMuxer.LSSTMongoMuxer

methods.name: ampel.lsst.ingest.LSSTMongoMuxer.LSSTMongoMuxer.process
documentation:
---
param dps_al: datapoints from alert
param stock_id: stock id from alert
---

Attempt to determine which pps/uls should be inserted into the t0 collection,
and which one should be marked as superseded.

This class compares info between alert and DB so that only the needed info is ingested later.

\# Not used - removed
\:param alert_history_length: alerts must not contain all available info for a given transient.
Alerts for LSST should provide a photometric history of 365 days.

JN: For *elasticc* diffimage and forced photometry will have identical flux
values. Since there is both a previous source phot and previous forced phot
history, a single alert can contain multiple copes of the same measurement.
Again, for *elasticc* it also seems like they keep the same id
ForcedSourceID and SourceID
for both kinds. As JN understand, this means that as new measurements come
in, the typical SourceId flux values will be retained and forced phot
"updates" not added to the DB. As the flux is the same this \_should\_ not
matter.
What we should do is probably:
\- Generate ID datapoints in LSSTDataPointShaper, prob of a hash from
[time, flux, ccdVisitId(?)].
\- Make a proper superseeded search a la ZiMongoMuxer here where we would
look for identical time (only?)
But since this should not make a difference, provided we drop duplicates in
the LSSTDataPointShaper it seems that this change is not strictly necessary
now (assuming I got everything right.)

methods.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.process
documentation:
Mandatory implementation.
To exclude the alert, return *None*
To accept it, either return
* self.on_match_t2_units
* or a custom combination of T2 unit names

class_variables.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.min_gal_lat
documentation: minium distance from galactic plane. Set to negative to disable cut.

documentation: search radius for GAIA DR2 matching [arcsec]

class_variables.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.gaia_pm_signif
documentation: significance of proper motion detection of GAIA counterpart [sigma]

class_variables.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.gaia_plx_signif
documentation: significance of parallax detection of GAIA counterpart [sigma]

class_variables.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.gaia_veto_gmag_min
documentation: min gmag for normalized distance cut of GAIA counterparts [mag]

class_variables.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.gaia_veto_gmag_max
documentation: max gmag for normalized distance cut of GAIA counterparts [mag]

class_variables.name: ampel.lsst.t0.SimpleLSSTFilter.SimpleLSSTFilter.gaia_excessnoise_sig_max
documentation: maximum allowed noise (expressed as significance) for Gaia match to be trusted.

General-purpose filter. It selects alerts based on:
* numper of previous detections
* distance to known SS objects
* detection of proper-motion and paralax for coincidence sources in GAIA DR2

methods.name: ampel.lsst.alert.ElasticcAlertSupplier.ElasticcAlertSupplier.__next__
documentation:
---
raises AttributeError: if alert_loader was not set properly before this method is
  called
raises StopIteration: when alert_loader dries out.
returns: a dict with a structure that AlertConsumer understands
---

Iterable class that, for each lightcurve provided by the underlying alert_loader,
returns an AmpelAlert instance.

classes.name: ampel.lsst.alert.load.ElasticcTrainingsetLoader.ElasticcLcIterator
documentation:
Iterator returns the next alert which would be generated from a lightcurve.

First idea:
- lightcurve is assumed to be an ELAsTICC AstropyTable where 'SIM_MAGOBS'
determines whether an alert was generated (otherwise 99)
Does not work, looks like all datapoints after the first detection have
SIM_MAGOBS.

Second idea:
- Straightforward significance. Need a [significance] detection to trigger
an alert.

classes.name: ampel.lsst.alert.load.ElasticcTrainingsetLoader.ElasticcTrainingsetLoader
documentation:
Load alerts from the ELAsTICC training set lightcurves.
These are assumed to be distributed in "SNANA" fits format:
- Simulated based on models.
- Two connected files ({file_path}_HEAD.FITS.gz, {file_path}_PHOT.FITS.gz)
- A PHOT file contains *full* lightcurves of transients.
- Each *lightcurve* will be broken into individual alerts.

Todo: Remove meta fields with NaN values (None, -9, -99, ...)
Not sure I know exactly how these are allocated, so skipping for now.

classes.name: ampel.lsst.alert.LSSTAlertSupplier.LSSTAlertSupplier

methods.name: ampel.lsst.alert.LSSTAlertSupplier.LSSTAlertSupplier.__next__
documentation:
---
raises AttributeError: if alert_loader was not set properly before this method is
  called
raises StopIteration: when alert_loader dries out.
returns: a dict with a structure that AlertConsumer understands
---

Iterable class that, for each alert payload provided by the underlying alert_loader,
returns an AmpelAlert instance.
